mnemonics.title: Covariance Sign Memory
mnemonics.content: P-A-N: Positive → Both Rise, Negative → Opposite, Zero → No pattern. Think: Pairs Always are Not correlated when zero (PAN).

mnemonics.title: Correlation Range
mnemonics.content: N-A-P: Negative (-1) to Positive (+1). NAP covers the full range. N for Negative, A for Always between, P for Positive.

mnemonics.title: Covariance vs Correlation
mnemonics.content: Covariance = Raw Relationship, Correlation = Standardized Relationship. Remember: Covariance can be any number, Correlation is Always unitless and between -1 and 1.

acronyms.usage: Used to determine if variables tend to move together (positive) or in opposite directions (negative)

acronyms.expansion: Pearson Correlation Coefficient - measures linear relationship strength and direction
acronyms.usage: Used to compare relationships between different variable pairs on a standardized scale

visualTips.visualization: Imagine two people walking together - when one steps forward, the other also steps forward. Draw an upward-sloping scatter plot.
Think of a see-saw - when one side goes up, the other goes down. Draw a downward-sloping scatter plot.
Picture random confetti scattered on a paper with no pattern. The points show no direction.
Think of a number line from -1 to +1: -1 (perfect negative), -0.5 (moderate negative), 0 (no linear), +0.5 (moderate positive), +1 (perfect positive)

analogies.analogy: Covariance is like a relationship status - it tells you if people are moving together but doesn't give you a measurable comparison between different pairs.
Correlation is like converting currencies - it normalizes the relationship so you can compare different pairs fairly regardless of their original scales.
Perfect correlation (r=±1) is like twins doing everything identically - one always mirrors the other's exact movements in a straight line.
